Suggestions For Microphones
And Recording Equipment
(Note: All prices and equipment sources are good as of December 2011. Sources, availability and price
may change. I do not make any commissions from these recommendations.)
First-Class (Studio-Quality)
I use a Studio Projects B1 microphone. Price: $119.95
http://www.zzounds.com/item--STUB1
This microphone requires what’s known as a phantom power source, so for that I use
the MobilePRE M-Audio preamp. Price: $149.99
http://www.zzounds.com/item--MDOMOBILEPREV2

-- 1 of 2 --

Extras I suggest: a pop filter (great for taking out Ps and spikes in your audio), a
microphone desktop stand, and a basic microphone cable. Price: Apx. $50
http://www.zzounds.com/item--MUPDS7425
http://www.zzounds.com/item--CBIMLC
http://www.zzounds.com/item--RAXPOMT
The MobilePRE connects to my computer via a USB cable, and comes with simple
software. Just a few clicks and you’re ready to go.
Total Investment: About $320 plus shipping/handling
Middle-of-the-Road (Still Good Quality)
An option that many people like is the Blue Yeti microphone. Price: Apx. $100
http://www.zzounds.com/item--BALYETI
This microphone comes with it’s own stand, has a mute button on the front, and records
at various angles. The sound quality is not a crisp as the First Class option, but it’s far
less expensive, and the quality is sufficient for any VSL recording you need to do. All
you need to connect this microphone to your computer is a basic USB cable.
Bare Bones Minimum
Simply record using your laptop or computer microphone. While the quality is not going
to match either of the two choices above, it will suffice in an economic pinch. Price: $0

-- 2 of 2 --
